subject: Public Health
title: The Human Right to Water
description: A quick win: understand The Human Right to Water.

hook.text: In 2010, the UN declared access to clean water a fundamental human right. Billions still lack it.

buildup.text: The right to water means sufficient, safe, acceptable, physically accessible, and affordable water for all.

discovery.text: Recognizing water as a right shifts the debate—governments become duty-bearers, not just service providers.

twist.text: Water privatization in some countries has raised prices—making the 'right' to water unaffordable for the poorest.

climax.text: Legal recognition alone isn't enough—enforcement mechanisms and funding must follow declarations.

punchline.text: A right without resources is just words on paper.

quiz.question: When did the UN declare access to clean water a human right?
